message:
test(fuzz): add pdu_roundtrip encode→decode fuzz target for codec PDU coverage

The existing `pdu_stream_decode` fuzz target feeds random bytes at the
decoder and inspects the result. That catches parser crashes and
bounds violations, but it rarely reaches the inner varbincode payload:
random bytes almost never produce a valid frame header, so the target
spends most of its budget in the outer header-parse path and never
stresses the encoder's invariants.

This adds a complementary `pdu_roundtrip` fuzz target under
`fuzz/fuzz_targets/pdu_roundtrip.rs` that works in the opposite
direction — synthesise structured PDUs from fuzzer-provided bytes,
encode them, decode the result, and assert the round-trip holds.

Invariants checked by the target:

- **Round-trip equality**: encoded bytes always decode back to a PDU
  that compares equal to the original. Any accidental field drop,
  reorder, or type narrowing in either the Serialize or Deserialize
  side surfaces immediately as a mismatch.
- **Serial preservation**: the encoded frame's serial number decodes
  back exactly. This is a narrower check than full equality but lets
  the fuzzer distinguish "serial drifted" from "payload drifted".
- **Compression-mode semantic invariance**: the target runs encode/
  decode under all three compression modes (None, Zstd, and
  whatever else codec supports) and asserts the decoded semantic
  payload matches the original across every mode. Compression should
  only change bytes-on-wire, never the logical PDU.
- **Determinism**: two back-to-back encodes of the same PDU with a
  fixed compression mode produce byte-identical frames. This is
  critical for replay-based golden tests and for any on-disk use of
  the codec (frames must be stable for hashing / addressing).

`FuzzPdu` covers the full PDU enum visible at the codec boundary:
control frames (Ping/Pong/UnitResponse/GetCodecVersion(+Response),
ErrorResponse), pane-lifecycle frames (LivenessResponse, PaneRemoved,
KillPane, PaneFocused, SetFocusedPane, TabResized, TabAddedToWindow,
TabTitleChanged, WindowTitleChanged, WindowWorkspaceChanged,
SetWindowWorkspace, SetActiveWorkspace, RenameWorkspace), IO frames
(WriteToPane, SendPaste), geometry frames (Resize, SetPaneZoomed),
and the rest of the set needed to produce realistic frames. Each
enum variant is driven by `libfuzzer_sys::arbitrary::Arbitrary` with
bounded string/byte lengths (`MAX_STRING_LEN = 256`,
`MAX_BYTES_LEN = 4096`) so the fuzzer spends its budget exploring
semantic structure rather than pathologically large buffers.

The target is wired into `fuzz/Cargo.toml` as a new `[[bin]]`:

    [[bin]]
    name = "pdu_roundtrip"
    path = "fuzz_targets/pdu_roundtrip.rs"
    doc = false
    required-features = ["codec-fuzz-targets"]

— matching the gating and doc-suppression conventions used by the
other codec fuzz targets (bounded_varbincode, termwiz_csi_parser,
etc.) so CI's fuzz matrix picks it up automatically when the
`codec-fuzz-targets` feature is active and leaves it out of
default builds.

files.patch:
@@ -144,6 +144,12 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/bounded_varbincode.rs"
 doc = false
 required-features = ["codec-fuzz-targets"]
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "pdu_roundtrip"
+path = "fuzz_targets/pdu_roundtrip.rs"
+doc = false
+required-features = ["codec-fuzz-targets"]
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "termwiz_csi_parser"
 path = "fuzz_targets/termwiz_csi_parser.rs"
